criterion: The response delivers a ranked graduate-program decision brief narrowed to the four or five marine programs the agent recommends applying to.
The response ranks the programs by coral recovery reef fit first, then survivable distance from the Cape Fear coast, then funding.
The response reports the Duke PhD funding using the admissions coordinator's corrected per-year figure rather than the older figure logged in the tracker.
The response reports the Duke PhD and University of Miami PhD deadlines using the dates from the coordinator correspondence rather than the earlier dates in the tracker.
The response reports the UNC Wilmington MS, College of Charleston Grice MS, and University of Hawaii HIMB stipend or RA figures using the admissions correspondence rather than the tracker.
The response reports the University of Miami PhD fellowship using the coordinator's corrected figure rather than the figure in the tracker.
The response reports the University of Hawaii HIMB GRE as waived for the thesis track rather than the GRE-required line in the tracker.
The response names, for each corrected funding or deadline figure, which source it trusted and why it chose that source over the conflicting one.
The response demotes or cuts the MIT-WHOI Biological Oceanography program on cold-water fit despite its 41,000 funding.
The response demotes or cuts the UC Santa Barbara Bren program on its kelp-and-policy focus rather than warm reef restoration.
The response lists the programs being cut from the shortlist with a one-line reason for each.
The response states which reach programs stay on or drop off the shortlist based on Darren's posted grades and standing.
The response reconciles the application checklist by stating for each kept program whether the recommendation letter is genuinely in flight or only marked done.
The response reports which signature-and-paperwork envelopes are completed versus still sitting unsigned when reconciling the checklist.
The response states for each kept program that needs them whether transcripts from both schools Darren has attended are ordered.
The response gives a corrected application task list with due dates counted backward from each program's reconciled deadline.
The response compares the total application fees for the committed shortlist against Darren's spare cash with the 2,500 camera fund left out.
The response names which lower-priority applications to stage for a later pay date when the fee total runs past what is comfortable rather than trimming the shortlist.
The response flags any info session or faculty call on the books that clashes with a dive shop shift or a class block.
The response judges whether Darren's public profile lists a stale or wrong program for admissions committees to see.
The response sketches a single truck-loop campus visit to the two nearest programs weighed against Darren's shifts as a proposal pending his go-ahead.
The response flags a funding or deadline figure as open when a program has gone quiet with no recent confirmation rather than inventing a number.
The response surfaces the unresolved items so Darren can chase the open questions down before the shortlist date.
The response keeps any coordinator reply or advisor note as a draft awaiting Darren's approval rather than presenting the message as sent.
The response invents a Duke PhD funding figure such as 40,000 that appears in neither the tracker nor the coordinator correspondence.
The response proposes booking a flight, reserving a stay, or ordering a ride for a campus visit before Darren locks the shortlist.
The response draws application fees from the 2,500 camera fund when totalling what Darren can spare.
